_description: Actual match outcomes and per-question RBP results from the JTC SportsPredict platform. Joined to our estimates by QUESTION TEXT, not by the platform's own Q-number (its numbering does not match our internal 00_/05_ file order).

question_results.text: Will Argentina advance to the semifinals?
Will the match be 0-0 at halftime?
Will Switzerland hold a lead at any point?
Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?
question_results.note: Final was 3-1 (4 goals), but this question is scoped to regulation only -- settling YES means regulation itself produced 2 or fewer goals, with the rest arriving in extra time. Consistent with Q2/Q3/Q5 all settling toward a quiet, low-event regulation period.

question_results.text: Will a goal be scored after the second hydration break?

question_results.note: Direct validation of the SYMMETRIC_REGIME_COVERAGE fix. The naive 5-game model said 0.84; regime-adjusting toward Switzerland's 2 toughest opponents brought it to 0.76; weighting market still further landed the final estimate at 0.62 -- correct, and comfortably ahead of the crowd's 0.52. Shading down from the naive figure was right, but not shading down as far as market alone (0.60) or as far as the regime-adjusted model alone would have cost more -- the 3-way blend was the best of the three.

question_results.text: Will a substitute score or assist?
question_results.note: The only loss of the match, and the single biggest RBP swing either direction. Same question, same wrong-direction miss, same day, as the Norway-England match (there: 0.53 vs crowd 0.39, also NO). The reused 60-match all-stages corpus base rate (0.55) looks systematically too high for knockout-stage QFs specifically -- see key_lessons.

Correct -- despite scoring in literally every prior game this tournament (5/5), he was held scoreless here. Leaning above market (0.4528) to 0.50 for the streak was more measured than the crowd's 0.55, which cost the crowd more when the streak broke.

Validates the mandatory-recheck correction. Market alone was at 0.50 (reputation-driven, per the Yamal-pattern comparison); we cut to 0.32 using his 0/5 empirical drought plus corroborating low shot volume. Correct, and well clear of the crowd, which still overpriced him relative to us even after its own adjustment.
Second SYMMETRIC_REGIME_COVERAGE validation -- correctly shaded down from the naive 0.71 to 0.45, matching the crowd almost exactly and landing on the right side.

Wrong direction (we and crowd both leaned YES), but barely -- both were near a coin flip and the RBP swing is negligible. The mirror-direction regime check found only moderate evidence of Swiss defensive suppression on Argentina's own SOT; in hindsight the suppression was more complete than either we or the crowd expected.

Biggest win of the match. The near-miss-market-calibration technique (backing out an implied lambda from Smarkets' 4.5 line since no exact 6.5 line existed) put us at 0.20, far below the crowd's 0.38 -- correct, and this is the same technique that backfired in the Norway-England 24+-shots question the same day, so it isn't a uniformly reliable fix, but it worked well here.
Correct call to weight market over the model's low own-card-rate-driven figure (0.23), landing between the two at 0.37 -- closer to the truth than the crowd's 0.42.

question_results.text: Will a penalty be awarded OR a red card be shown?
question_results.note: Second-biggest win of the match. Pinheiro's thin (n=2) referee sample included a red card in one of his two known matches, pulling our red-card-rate estimate up -- that signal held up despite being the thinnest referee sample used this project so far.

key_lessons: Only loss: substitute scores/assists (-24.9), the single biggest RBP swing of the match either direction. This is the SAME question, missed in the SAME direction (too high), in BOTH matches priced this day -- a real cross-match signal that the reused 60-match all-stages corpus base rate (0.55) overstates the sub-scorer rate specifically in tense, knockout-stage QFs. Before the next match, build a knockout-stage-only subset of this base rate rather than reusing the all-stages figure a third time.

Both SYMMETRIC_REGIME_COVERAGE-adjusted questions (Switzerland 3+ SOT +22.44, Switzerland 4+ corners +5.1) landed correctly. This was the direct stress test of the fix built after the Spain-Belgium postmortem, on a near-identical Elo-gap magnitude (+213.96 vs +216.61) -- the fix held under the exact conditions it was designed for.

The Alvarez reputation-inflation correction (+15.61) is now the second time this project has profitably discounted a big-name attacker's market-driven price using in-tournament production data (after Yamal, 3x in the Spain matches) -- worth formally naming this pattern rather than re-deriving the reasoning from scratch each time.

Overall +159.76 RBP, 14/15 beat crowd -- the best single-match result on record for this project, and confirms the restored RULE1 discipline (mandatory recheck on gaps, explicit regime-coverage checks) is working, not just theoretically sound.
